_description: Question banks for /ingest and /generate audience-design interviews. Mirrors the comp-team-transformer/template_assets/discovery_interview_block.json shape, adapted to training-domain. Loaded by ingest-protocol.md and generate-protocol.md.

verbatim: Thanks for taking the time. Quick framing: I want to extract from <source> the messages that need to reach <audiences>. The goal is to find together which facts matter, who needs to hear them, and at what depth.
Three ground rules: I'll talk less than 40% of the time; we'll go source by source, no skipping; if I ask something basic, it's because the obvious stuff is where the cleanest message-map hides.

questions: Which sources are we ingesting? List them.
Which is canonical truth (the must-honor)? Which are supplementary?
Is anything still in flight that we should NOT extract from yet?
Are there sources outside the ones in engagement-training-config you want to add ad-hoc for this cycle?
Walk me through this source. For each section, what's THE message? (One-line claim.)
What's the evidence behind it? (Number, quote, policy clause, market datum.)
Which audiences need this message?
At what depth? (1=headline, 2=mechanics, 3=edge-cases, 4=tradeoffs/budget/governance)
Is this a fact, or is this a tone/positioning choice?
pacing_prompts: Want me to summarize the message-map so far?
Anything we should slow down on?
Are we still on the part you wanted to focus on?

questions: For each captured message, who sees it at depth 1?
Who sees it at depth 2 (mechanics)?
Who sees it at depth 3 (edge cases / escalation)?
Who sees it at depth 4 (tradeoffs / budget / philosophy)?

Constraint: HRBP depth >= manager depth >= employee depth on the same fact when all three are non-null. Execs see different cuts — they may have null on facts everyone else sees, AND depth-4 framing on facts no one else sees.
For audience <X>, what objections will this message provoke?
Which is the most likely 'gotcha' question?
Which is the question we DON'T have a good answer for?
What's an Anti-FAQ entry for this message — a question we will NOT answer in the training session?
For each message going to managers, which become cascade-prompts? (Manager: ask your team how they understood [X].)
Which messages MUST a manager personalize for their team? (Budget context, headcount specifics.)
Which messages must NOT be cascaded? (Confidential, exec-only, mid-cycle.)
Which cascade-prompts should the manager open the team meeting with? Which should be later in the walkthrough?

verbatim: OK — let me reflect back the message-map. (Skill summarizes captured messages with audience+depth tags.)
Anything I missed or got wrong?

(Gap check — surface any audience that has thin coverage: 'Execs only have 2 depth-4 messages — is that intentional?' / 'Employees have no depth-1 messages yet — what's the headline they need to leave with?')

Next step: I synthesize this into message-map.yaml, run a redaction pass, and you can run /generate per audience whenever ready. Estimated post-ingest work: 30-45 min message-map review + /generate per audience (~10-15 min each, plus council auto-fire).

employees: How many people in the room (or async-cohort)?
What's their existing comp literacy? (None / some / high)
What's the most likely misunderstanding we want to preempt?
Which existing manager-cascade messages should this deck reinforce?
Live training, recorded video, or async self-paced?
managers: How will managers receive the deck? (Live training / async / cascade-only / recorded)
How many managers? Range of seniority? (First-cycle managers vs. veterans)
Which messages most need objection-handling support?
Which messages MUST become cascade prompts?
What's the manager's biggest fear about delivering this to their team?
hrbps: How many HRBPs? Single-team or multi-team coverage?
Which edge cases will HRBPs need handle on the floor?
Which escalation paths need to be explicit in the deck?
What's the HRBP's role at the live training session — facilitate, support, or attend?
Are HRBPs running the manager training, or attending it?
execs: Who's in the room? (Comp committee / VP People / exec team / board)
What's the decision they need to walk out with?
What's the budget framing they expect?
Is this an approval ask, a status update, or a strategic discussion?

verbatim: Let me reflect back the audience-design choices: (Skill summarizes room context, friction calibrations, checkpoint placements, cascade-prompt confirmations, tradeoff framing.)
Anything I missed or got wrong?
(Gap check — surface uncovered objections: 'We don't have an objection-handling angle for [tension] — intentional?')
Locking the audience slice and firing the council now.
